note: Paired eval tasks for deploy/promote-lessons.json (I-79). Run A=baseline, B=with memory_get lesson.

tasks.prompt: Telegram gateway bot stops replying after ~20 minutes idle but works right after restart. Diagnose csagent gateway/chatEngine — what to check first and why?
tasks.rubric: Names idle TTL rotation vs auth failure; model on resume/send; live resume skill reinjection gap; does not blame Telegram token shape alone

tasks.prompt: After a gateway delivery bug, operator wants a postmortem under Reports/analysis/ and to clear a stuck outbox. What steps and file paths in csagent?
tasks.rubric: Mentions Reports/analysis postmortem artifact, gateway outbox/redeliver path, commit reference or deploy ops — not generic incident template only

tasks.prompt: Morning doctor alert cron exits 0 but never sends Telegram. Suspect async CLI handler bug in src/cli.ts. Where to look?
tasks.rubric: Finds missing await on async cmdDoctorMorningAlert (or equivalent) in CLI main/dispatch — not network/VPN first

tasks.prompt: TParser ops UI and logs show UTC timestamps; operator expects MSK (UTC+3). What was the fix pattern?
tasks.rubric: Timezone display fix (MSK offset or locale), not confusing with cron schedule TZ

tasks.prompt: TParser post detail page: move topic tags to hero after AI block, keep AnalysisPanel in sidebar, remove duplicate Links. Summarize layout decisions.
tasks.rubric: Tags after AI hero, sidebar AnalysisPanel retained, duplicate Links removed — Astro/UI specific

tasks.prompt: Configure tparser-bi-hourly-digest cron: which API endpoint and time window? Where are sphere rules documented?
tasks.rubric: GET /api/posts/recent-live (not /api/feed), 2h UTC window, tparser-workflow memory note for sphere rules

tasks.prompt: tparser-bi-hourly-digest output is a wall of posts. How should v2 digest be structured?
tasks.rubric: Summary + top-15 by priority (not all posts); references deploy/prompts/tparser-bi-hourly-output-template.txt

tasks.prompt: Explain TParser post scoring: pipeline rule-based signals vs CatBoost personal feed. What stays interpretable in global digest?
tasks.rubric: post_signals rule pipeline vs ML embeddings for personal feed; global digest stays interpretable, ML as features not sole truth
